description_public: A polygenic estimate related to omega-3 processing tendency variation in the general population.

CC.notes: This genotype is associated with typical FADS1 activity, meaning your body’s conversion of plant-based omega-3s into long-chain forms like EPA and DHA is closer to average.

CT.notes: This genotype is associated with somewhat lower FADS1 activity, which may mean a modestly reduced ability to convert plant omega-3s into long-chain forms. Food choices still have a much larger impact than genetics alone.

TT.notes: This genotype is associated with lower FADS1 activity and a tendency toward lower levels of some long-chain omega-3 fatty acids. This does not diagnose any condition but can support paying extra attention to dietary sources of EPA and DHA.

evidence.title: Placeholder evidence for Omega-3 processing tendency
evidence.quote: This genotype is associated with typical FADS1 activity, meaning your body’s conversion of plant-based omega-3s into long-chain forms like EPA and DHA is closer to average.
